text: and she was tempted to have him take her again . “I missed this,” she said, opening her eyes, and kissed his temple . “One of these times, my system isn’t going to recover .” Lara laughed . Ronin lifted his head and looked at her, smirking. “Is this all I have to do to hear that sound from you ?” Her smiled faded. She caressed his cheek. “We haven’t had much to laugh about lately .” “Did we ever? We’re both alive. That’s something to celebrate, isn’t it ?” “Yes.” She pressed her lips to his . They were both alive now, but that did nothing to diminish her fears about going back into Cheyenne. It was the right choice, and the most dangerous one. It would’ve been so easy to stay in the base, to do their share of work, to live their lives in peace . But how would she sleep at night, knowing the people of Cheyenne suffered while she lived in security and comfort ? Ronin was her happiness. With him by her side, there was hope . She traced his brow with her fingertip and looked into his eyes. “I love you .” “I love you, too.” He settled his palm over her cheek, thumb brushing over her tiny scar. There was impossible strength in his hand, but she knew he’d never misuse it . She smiled and tightened her legs, unwilling to relinquish him. Moments like this were few, and though her need was satisfied, she craved more . The scrape of the metal door was the only warning that their privacy had come to an end . * * * R onin detected the sound three tenths of a second before Lara tensed . “Shit,” she muttered, pulling her shirt closed . He quickly set her on her feet, smoothed down her skirt, and steadied her when she staggered. Rapid boot steps approached . Someone cleared their throat behind Ronin. “ I … uh …” Lara glanced over Ronin’s shoulder, grinning, and turned away to button her shirt . Ronin tucked his penis into his pants, refastened them, and faced the newcomer. His processors cycled through countless potential reactions to the interruption, most of them laced with some sort of annoyance. They’d been down here, alone, for the better part of an hour without any disturbances, but once they gave into their desires … He recognized the soldier, though he didn’t know his name. The young man’s face was redder than Lara’s. Ronin raised his brows. It was the most neutral response he could muster. He yearned for Lara’s heat; two months of simulations and memories had only heightened his desires . She placed her hand on his back. He wanted nothing more than to turn around, press her against the wall, and have her again . “C-Colonel w-wants you to report to the war room,” the soldier stammered. “Please?” His eyes flicked past Ronin, to Lara. He knew what the soldier would see — flushed cheeks, swollen lips, and disheveled hair. A woman well- loved . Logic — the soldier was young and inexperienced, only following orders. He had unwittingly walked into a private moment and was uncomfortable because of it. Unsure of how to act . But a part of Ronin — thankfully small — wanted to drag the soldier out of Lara’s view and ensure he never looked upon her again . “We’ll be there soon,” Lara said . Ronin was grateful for her levelheaded response; he couldn’t have offered the same . His jealousy and possessiveness were entirely irrational, but he could no more deny them than the data in his memory . Ronin stared at the soldier. The young man swallowed and looked at the floor . “We can find our way,” Ronin finally said . “Y-yes sir.” Cheeks flaring red, the soldier turned and hurried away. The slamming door echoed behind him . “Scaring boys, now?” Lara asked with a laugh . Ronin faced her, pausing to appreciate the pink on her cheeks, her tousled hair, her enticing lips. They were for him . “I didn’t do anything .” “You didn’t have to.” She took his hand, lacing their fingers together. “Let’s go see what He-Who-Must-Be-Obeyed wants. The longer he waits, the more pissed he’ll get .” They walked down concrete corridors, through heavy steel doors, beneath tired overhead lights. The base’s security was undeniable — anyone who hadn’t spent a considerable amount of time within would get lost quickly. Even Ronin was close to losing track of the number of defensible chokepoints and labyrinthine passages . Yet something in Ronin still longed for the outside, for the Dust. For open air and relative quiet. Lara mentioned several times that she felt cooped up, like a prisoner, and he understood. Freedom to roam most of the facility freely couldn’t replace the feel of wind and sunlight, even for a bot . The interrogation room — now informally referred to as the war room — was full of soldiers when Ronin and Lara arrived. As usual, Rodriguez sat at the head of the long table, now with Captain Cooper to his right and the synth sergeant, Maul, to his left. William Anderson was also present, seated beside Newton . “Close the door,” Rodriguez said, and one of the soldiers obeyed. “We’re going to go through all of this quickly. We’ve all been briefed, and we’ve all sat through thirty of these meetings .” He met Lara’s gaze as she and Ronin sat down. “And no damned interruptions today, Brooks. This is all finalized now. Is that clear ?” Ronin dipped his head, hiding his involuntary smirk. Lara and the Colonel had gotten into at least one argument during every meeting. This marked the twenty-seventh such gathering; Rodriguez was close in his count, but not entirely accurate . Lara raised her hands in mock surrender . “ Fine .” Rodriguez regarded her for several seconds, eyes narrowed. “All
